revision.text: #redirect[[en:Saito_Kofun_Festival]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''西都古墳まつり'''とは11月最初の土･日の二日間にかけて西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社でも開催される[[祭り]]である。１日目には[[神楽]]まつり・松明行列・炎の祭典などが、２日目には式典・奉納行事などが執り行われる。
市の職員が古墳時代の服装をしたり、国[[重要文化財]]の神楽・[[臼太鼓踊り]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
[[category:日本の祭り|* まつり]]

'''西都古墳まつり'''とは11月最初の土･日の二日間にかけて、[[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社でも開催される[[祭り]]である。１日目には[[神楽]]まつり・松明行列・炎の祭典などが、２日目には式典・奉納行事などが執り行われる。
市の職員が古墳時代の服装をしたり、国[[重要文化財]]の神楽・[[臼太鼓踊り]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
[[category:日本の祭り|* まつり]]

'''西都古墳まつり'''とは11月最初の土･日の二日間にかけて、[[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社でも開催される[[祭り]]である。１日目には[[神楽]]まつり・松明行列・炎の祭典などが、２日目には式典・奉納行事などが執り行われる。
市の職員が古墳時代の服装をしたり、国[[重要文化財]]の神楽・[[下水流臼太鼓踊]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
[[category:日本の祭り|* まつり]]

'''西都古墳まつり'''とは[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社などで分散して開催される[[祭り]]である。西都市中心部から西に数百mの位置に広がる西都原[[古墳]]群にちなむ。祭りの由来自体は約600年前にさかのぼるが、現在の形で祝うようになったのは昭和末からである。開催月は11月。第一土曜日と翌日の日曜日の2日間にわたって開催される。
1日目の内容は、[[神楽]]まつり、松明行列、炎の祭典を中心とする。2日目は式典と奉納行事などを執り行う。
市の職員が古墳時代の服装をしたり、国[[重要文化財]]の神楽・[[下水流臼太鼓踊]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
[[category:日本の祭り|* まつり]]

'''西都古墳まつり'''とは[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社などで分散して開催される[[祭り]]である。西都市中心部から西に数百mの位置に広がる[[西都原古墳群]]にちなむ。祭りの由来自体は約600年前に西都原台地と市街地の中間にある三宅神社記録の残る[[天孫降臨]]祭・山陵祭にまで遡るが、現在の形で執り行うようになったのは昭和末からである。毎年11月の第一土曜日と翌日の日曜日の2日間にわたって開催される。
1日目の内容は、[[神楽]]まつり、松明行列・炎の祭典を中心とする。2日目は式典と奉納行事などを執り行う。
市民や市の職員が古墳時代の服装をして行う松明行列や市の職員が[[日向神話]]のうち[[ニニギノミコト]]と[[コノハナサクヤヒメ]]に関する話を再現する炎の祭典（この際、ニニギノミコトとコノハナサクヤヒメを演じる職員は独身でなければならない）が行われ、国[[重要文化財]]の神楽・[[下水流臼太鼓踊]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
[[category:日本の祭り|* まつり]]

[[Category:宮崎県の祭り]]

[[ファイル:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|サムネイル|西都古墳まつり・炎の祭典]] '''西都古墳まつり'''（'''さいとこふんまつり'''）とは[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社などで分散して開催される[[祭り]]である。西都市中心部から西に数百mの位置に広がる[[西都原古墳群]]にちなむ。祭りの由来自体は約600年前に西都原台地と市街地の中間にある三宅神社記録の残る[[天孫降臨]]祭・山陵祭にまで遡るが、現在の形で執り行うようになったのは昭和末からである。毎年11月の第一土曜日と翌日の日曜日の2日間にわたって開催される。
1日目の内容は、[[神楽]]まつり、[[松明]]行列・炎の祭典を中心とする。2日目は式典と奉納行事などを執り行う。
市民や市の職員が古墳時代の服装をして行う松明行列や市民が日向神話のうち[[ニニギノミコト]]と[[コノハナサクヤヒメ]]に関する話を再現する炎の祭典（この際、ニニギノミコトとコノハナサクヤヒメを演じる職員は独身でなければならない）が行われ、国の選択無形民俗文化財である神楽・[[下水流臼太鼓踊]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
{{デフォルトソート:さいとこふんまつり}}
[[Category:宮崎県の祭り]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|Thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Fire Festival]] The '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ('''Saito Kofun Matsuri''') is a [[festival]] held in [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], primarily centered around Saitobaru but also distributed across locations such as Tsuma Shrine and other areas in the city. The festival is associated with the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], located a few hundred meters west of Saito City’s central area. The origins of the..."
revision.text:
[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|Thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Fire Festival]] The '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ('''Saito Kofun Matsuri''') is a [[festival]] held in [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], primarily centered around Saitobaru but also distributed across locations such as Tsuma Shrine and other areas in the city. The festival is associated with the [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]], located a few hundred meters west of Saito City’s central area. The origins of the festival date back approximately 600 years to the Tenjin Descending Festival and Mausoleum Festival recorded at Miyake Shrine, which is located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city center. However, the festival in its current form has been held since the late Showa era. It is conducted over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November each year.
The first day's events include a [[kagura]] festival, a torch parade, and the Fire Festival. On the second day, ceremonies and offering rituals are performed.
A torchlight procession, where citizens and city officials dress in clothing from the Kofun period, is a highlight of the festival. Another key event is the Fire Festival, which reenacts the story of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]] from the Hyuga myth. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be performed by unmarried city employees. Additionally, traditional performing arts from Saito City, such as the kagura dance and the [[Shitadori Usu Taiko Dance]] (a nationally designated intangible folk cultural property), are offered.
The festival also provides a rare opportunity to enter the grounds of [[Osahozuka]] and [[Mesahozuka]], which are typically closed to the public as they are [[Imperial mausoleum references]].
[[Category:Festivals in Miyazaki Prefecture]]

[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka|ja|女狭穂塚}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Saito Kofun Festival}} [[Category:Festivals in Miyazaki Prefecture]]

[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka|ja|女狭穂塚}}, which are usually off-limits as [[Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]].

[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. [[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns}}, which are usually off-limits as [[Imperial Mausoleum Reference Site|Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]].
{{DEFAULTSORT:Saito Kofun Festival}} [[Category:Festivals in Miyazaki Prefecture]]

The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Mausoleum Reference Sites}}

The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Mausoleum Reference Sites}}

A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Mausoleum Reference Sites}}

The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson Kōrin|ja|天孫降臨|12=draft}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=draft}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=draft}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=draft}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=draft}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=draft}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Mausoleum Reference Sites|12=draft}}
<!--Saito Kofun Festival-->

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:西都古墳まつり up to rev 95214664
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|サムネイル|西都古墳まつり・炎の祭典]] '''西都古墳まつり'''（'''さいとこふんまつり'''）とは[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の西都原を中心に妻市街地や都萬神社などで分散して開催される[[祭り]]である。西都市中心部から西に数百mの位置に広がる[[西都原古墳群]]にちなむ。祭りの由来自体は約600年前に西都原台地と市街地の中間にある三宅神社記録の残る[[天孫降臨]]祭・山陵祭にまで遡るが、現在の形で執り行うようになったのは昭和末からである。毎年11月の第一土曜日と翌日の日曜日の2日間にわたって開催される。
1日目の内容は、[[神楽]]まつり、[[松明]]行列・炎の祭典を中心とする。2日目は式典と奉納行事などを執り行う。
市民や市の職員が古墳時代の服装をして行う松明行列や市民が日向神話のうち[[ニニギノミコト]]と[[コノハナサクヤヒメ]]に関する話を再現する炎の祭典（この際、ニニギノミコトとコノハナサクヤヒメを演じる職員は独身でなければならない）が行われ、国の選択無形民俗文化財である神楽・[[下水流臼太鼓踊]]をはじめとする西都市の伝統芸能が奉納される。
日頃は[[陵墓参考地]]のため立ち入りが禁止されている[[男狭穂塚]]・[[女狭穂塚]]の敷地内に入ることができる貴重な機会でもある。
{{デフォルトソート:さいとこふんまつり}}
[[Category:宮崎県の祭り]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Saito Kofun Festival]][[ja:西都古墳まつり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11629710]] Wikidata name: Saito Kofun Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saito Kofun Festival|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the [[:en:Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]] Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|qq=|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}

The first day features events such as a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}

The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}

The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the [[:en:Tenson kōrin|Tenson Kōrin]] Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] performances.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Saito Kofun Festival]][[ja:西都古墳まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Saito Kofun Festival]][[ja:西都古墳まつり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Saito Kofun Festival]][[ja:西都古墳まつり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629710]] Wikidata name: Saito Kofun Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saito Kofun Festival|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|西都古墳まつり|version=95214664|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|西都古墳まつり|version=95214664|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saito Kofun Festival|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}

[[File:Kofunmatsuri.jpg|thumbnail|Saito Kofun Festival – Flame Ceremony]] '''Saito Kofun Festival''' ({{ill|Saito Kofun Festival|ja|西都古墳まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saito Kofun Festival|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is a {{ill|Festival in Japan|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held primarily in {{ill|Saitobaru|ja|西都原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitobaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture. The festival also takes place in other venues such as the city center of Saito, {{ill|Tsuma Shrine|ja|都萬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuma Shrine|qq=}}, and the surrounding areas. The event is linked to the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which is situated several hundred meters west of the city center.
The festival’s origins date back approximately 600 years to records of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} Festival and Imperial Mausoleum Festival at Miyake Shrine, located between the Saitobaru Plateau and the city. However, the festival took its current form in the late Shōwa era. It is held annually over two days on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.
The first day features events such as a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} festival, a torch procession, and the Flame Ceremony. On the second day, ceremonial events and dedication rituals take place.
The festival includes a torch procession, where local citizens and city employees dressed in period clothing from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} march through the area. A highlight is the Flame Ceremony, in which city employees reenact a myth from the {{ill|Himuka Mythology|ja|日向神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuka Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the story of {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナサクヤヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. The roles of Ninigi-no-Mikoto and Konohanasakuya-hime must be played by unmarried employees. The festival also features traditional performing arts from Saito, including the nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property {{ill|Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|ja|下水流臼太鼓踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Usudaiko Dance|1=Q11361053|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361053}} and other local {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} performances.
The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|西都古墳まつり|version=95214664|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The festival is one of the few opportunities to access the grounds of the {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka|ja|男狭穂塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 男狭穂塚古墳}} and {{ill|Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja|女狭穂塚|lt=Mezasahozuka Kofuns|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mezasahozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女狭穂塚古墳}}, which are usually off-limits as Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|lt=Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}
